summary: Iteration 2 — feature additions (push, kitchen ticket, bill splitter) tested. Backend: 19/19 pytest cases pass (17 regression + 2 new). Push relay endpoint /api/register-push returns an informative 5xx JSON with placeholder EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY (expected preview behaviour, no crash). Status transitions to 'ready' and 'served' return 200 even when push relay is unavailable (non-blocking wrap verified). All original endpoints (rooms CRUD, categories, items, orders CRUD, stats, WS order_created broadcast, regenerate cascade) remain green. Frontend push playbook checklist — all four handlers present at module scope in app/_layout.tsx (setNotificationHandler, setNotificationChannelAsync, addNotificationResponseReceivedListener, getLastNotificationResponseAsync); src/lib/push.ts uses getDevicePushTokenAsync and POSTs to /api/register-push; app.json plugins include 'expo-notifications' and ios.bundleIdentifier='com.emergent.servesync'; waiter pair.tsx invokes registerForPush(code,name) after successful pair. Frontend UI: Tablet Orders active-card shows [Edit] [Ticket] [X cancel] and Ready-column adds Finish button; served card shows [Ticket] [Split bill]. KitchenTicket modal renders correctly with restaurant name, Table, Waiter, Time, Order #, itemised lines, Notes block, TOTAL, and Print + Save-as-PDF action buttons (visually verified via screenshot).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: register-push maps upstream 401 -> HTTPException(500, informative) and 5xx -> 502; send_push is fire-and-forget wrapped in try/except inside update_order_status, so push failures never break the status transition (verified by test_status_ready_served_non_blocking_push).
push.ts correctly uses getDevicePushTokenAsync (native APNs/FCM token) per Emergent push playbook — not getExpoPushTokenAsync.

app/_layout.tsx places setNotificationHandler and setNotificationChannelAsync at module scope (before any component renders) which matches the playbook; addNotificationResponseReceivedListener + getLastNotificationResponseAsync are wired inside a useEffect and both branch on deeplink|action_url with http vs router.push routing.
KitchenTicket has a web-only fallback that opens the HTML in a new tab when Print.printAsync fails (good) and uses expo-print + expo-sharing for Save-as-PDF.

BillSplitter: tip is calculated as Math.round(subtotal * tipPct)/100 which rounds tip to nearest cent; totals per person in By-items mode redistribute tip proportionally to each person's item subtotal — correct behaviour.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of iteration-2 feature checks (playbook grep + Ticket modal render + active/served action bars)

seed_data_creation: Two transient rooms created ('UI_Ticket_Test', 'UI_Ticket_Test2') plus several TEST_ rooms via pytest fixtures; each auto-seeds default menu.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push cannot be end-to-end verified in preview (placeholder key -> upstream 401 -> our 500). Backend base URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Tests: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (19 tests, ~4s). UI seeding: localStorage 'servesync.role' + 'servesync.code' as JSON strings then navigate to /tablet or /waiter. Note: /tablet Orders board loads with active_only=true so served orders only appear via order_updated WebSocket events after the page is already open.
